title: carex nigra subsp. recta
intro: Carex nigra subsp. recta, also known as the black sedge, is a perennial grass-like plant in the sedge family (Cyperaceae). It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing inflammation, improving respiratory issues, and supporting digestive health. The black sedge can be prepared into various herbal preparations, including infusions, decoctions, and salves, which can be used topically or ingested to harness its medicinal properties.
